results.title: From Technological Substitution to Institutional Response: A Systematic Review of Anxiety, Resistance, and Governance Transformation among Low Skilled Workers in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
results.tldr: This review highlights clear gaps in existing research, including limited evidence from developing-country contexts, insufficient attention to within-occupation heterogeneity, an incomplete account of the psychological mechanisms underlying AI anxiety, and a shortage of rigorous evaluations of reskilling policy effectiveness; future research should strengthen cross-national comparisons, longitudinal tracking, and interdisciplinary collaboration to support the development of a technology governance framework that balances efficiency with equity.
results.abstract: This review highlights clear gaps in existing research, including limited evidence from developing-country contexts, insufficient attention to within-occupation heterogeneity, an incomplete account of the psychological mechanisms underlying AI anxiety, and a shortage of rigorous evaluations of reskilling policy effectiveness; future research should strengthen cross-national comparisons, longitudinal tracking, and interdisciplinary collaboration to support the development of a technology governance framework that balances efficiency with equity.

results.title: The Integration of Artificial Intelligence in Engineering Design, Manufacturing, and CAD: A Triangular Revolution of Innovation, Efficiency, and Automation
results.venue: Global Journal of Engineering and Technology Advances
results.tldr: This report explores the use of AI in manufacturing, focusing on its application in predictive maintenance, quality control, robotics, and process optimization.
results.abstract: This report explores the use of AI in manufacturing, focusing on its application in predictive maintenance, quality control, robotics, and process optimization.

results.title: THE ECONOMICS OF ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE: THE IMPACT OF AUTOMATION ON EMPLOYMENT AND GDP STRUCTURE
results.tldr: The article analyzes the macro- and microeconomic consequences of the introduction of AI and automation: increased efficiency, the emergence of new industries and an increase in the contribution of digital technologies to GDP; on the other, the risk of displacement of workers, especially in routine professions, and increased inequality.
results.abstract: The article analyzes the macro- and microeconomic consequences of the introduction of AI and automation: increased efficiency, the emergence of new industries and an increase in the contribution of digital technologies to GDP; on the other, the risk of displacement of workers, especially in routine professions, and increased inequality.

results.title: Towards Autonomous Collaborative Robot Assistance in Stationary Tooling Tasks: Lessons Learned from Empirical Evaluations in VR
results.venue: 2025 9th International Conference on Robotics and Automation Sciences (ICRAS)

results.title: Introducing Brain-Computer Interfaces in Factories and Fabrication Lines for the Inclusion of Disabled Workers-Industry 5.0 - A Modern Challenge and Opportunity
results.tldr: The study synthesizes interdisciplinary data from ergonomics, industrial automation, and EU regulatory frameworks to establish a conceptual model for human-machine interaction and proposes a six-step integration framework that links task demands, worker capabilities, and interaction modalities within human-in-the-loop manufacturing environments.
results.abstract: The study synthesizes interdisciplinary data from ergonomics, industrial automation, and EU regulatory frameworks to establish a conceptual model for human-machine interaction and proposes a six-step integration framework that links task demands, worker capabilities, and interaction modalities within human-in-the-loop manufacturing environments.

results.title: Navigating the Automation Conundrum in the Modern Workplace
results.venue: Challenges in the Turbulent Economic Environment and Organizations’ Sustainable Development

results.title: AI and Automation: Effects on Employment and Management
results.tldr: A typology of employment impact, ranging from automation-intensive displacement to augmentation-driven productivity gains, is proposed, arguing that the future of work depends not only on technological capability but on proactive policy, inclusive design, and agile management strategies.
results.abstract: A typology of employment impact, ranging from automation-intensive displacement to augmentation-driven productivity gains, is proposed, arguing that the future of work depends not only on technological capability but on proactive policy, inclusive design, and agile management strategies.

results.title: UNVEILING THE CHALLENGES AND OPPORTUNITIES IN THE CONTEXT OF EMPLOYMENT IN CHANGING LANDSCAPE OF ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE: A STUDY WITH REFERENCE TO MANUFACTURING INDUSTRY
results.tldr: Whether AI will mainly reduce jobs or act as a tool to support human abilities in the future is looked at.
results.abstract: Whether AI will mainly reduce jobs or act as a tool to support human abilities in the future is looked at.

results.title: Impact of Automation and AI on Employment in India: An Exploratory Report
results.tldr: The findings show that while automation and AI threaten routine, middle-skill, and clerical jobs, they simultaneously generate new opportunities in high-skill, knowledge-intensive, and hybrid roles.
results.abstract: The findings show that while automation and AI threaten routine, middle-skill, and clerical jobs, they simultaneously generate new opportunities in high-skill, knowledge-intensive, and hybrid roles.

results.title: The Competitive Trap: How AI-Driven Automation Creates Collective Market Failure
results.tldr: It is demonstrated that competitive pressure traps rational, forward-looking firms in an automation arms race that exceeds collectively optimal levels, and policy discourse should shift from managing displacement consequences to correcting the competitive incentives driving excessive automation.
results.abstract: It is demonstrated that competitive pressure traps rational, forward-looking firms in an automation arms race that exceeds collectively optimal levels, and policy discourse should shift from managing displacement consequences to correcting the competitive incentives driving excessive automation.

results.title: Creation, validation, obsolescence: observed evidence of AI-driven labor market displacement, 2020–2025
results.tldr: Obsable labor market data, while constrained by short observation windows, already document patterns consistent with AI-driven displacement rather than mere transformation—concentrated among routine cognitive tasks and junior roles, with preliminary but material evidence that developing economies reliant on cognitive services outsourcing face disproportionate disruption through both direct exposure and indirect demand-erosion channels.
results.abstract: Obsable labor market data, while constrained by short observation windows, already document patterns consistent with AI-driven displacement rather than mere transformation—concentrated among routine cognitive tasks and junior roles, with preliminary but material evidence that developing economies reliant on cognitive services outsourcing face disproportionate disruption through both direct exposure and indirect demand-erosion channels.

results.title: When the Escape Routes Close: Why AI-Driven Displacement May Break the Historical Pattern
results.tldr: It is argued that AI's simultaneous advancement across cognitive, perceptual, and increasingly physical domains is closing both historical "escape routes"—skill transferability and domain switching—faster than labor markets can adapt.
results.abstract: It is argued that AI's simultaneous advancement across cognitive, perceptual, and increasingly physical domains is closing both historical "escape routes"—skill transferability and domain switching—faster than labor markets can adapt.

results.title: Digital Technology Integration for Intelligent Industrial Decision-Making: An Empirical Investigation
authors: Parul Kashyap, Sunny Gaur, Ashu Pal
results.venue: International Journal of Advanced Research in Science, Communication and Technology
results.tldr: The research thoroughly examines upcoming challenges, including cybersecurity vulnerabilities, data privacy concerns, and the requirement to upskill workers to adapt to shifting demands, and the findings show that decision outcomes are now more accurate, responsive, and sustainable.
results.abstract: The research thoroughly examines upcoming challenges, including cybersecurity vulnerabilities, data privacy concerns, and the requirement to upskill workers to adapt to shifting demands, and the findings show that decision outcomes are now more accurate, responsive, and sustainable.

results.title: Does AI Enhance or Replace Human Productivity? Evidence from Service and Manufacturing Sectors
results.venue: International Journal of Multidisciplinary Research and Growth Evaluation
results.tldr: Evidence from service-sector experiments shows that AI can substantially increase productivity in customer service, professional writing, software development, consulting, and administrative work, particularly when tasks are structured, information-intensive, and supported by clear performance metrics, but gains are uneven and depend on worker skill, task type, data quality, organizational redesign, and human judgment.
results.abstract: Evidence from service-sector experiments shows that AI can substantially increase productivity in customer service, professional writing, software development, consulting, and administrative work, particularly when tasks are structured, information-intensive, and supported by clear performance metrics, but gains are uneven and depend on worker skill, task type, data quality, organizational redesign, and human judgment.

results.title: Technology-Enabled Work Practices and Workforce Retention in Postpandemic Manufacturing
results.venue: International journal of research and innovation in social science

results.title: Industrial Automation's Impact on Workforce: A Review in the USA and Africa
results.venue: International Journal of Multidisciplinary Futuristic Development

results.title: Mobility Restrictions and Automation in the Developing World: Evidence from Peru's Labor Market
